extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won his third Vuelta stage after CRADDOCK Lawson drove tempo all day in breakaway, delivering perfect leadout setup.
tactical_patterns: CRADDOCK Lawson pulled entire final kilometers of breakaway to set up CORT Magnus, eliminating counter-attacks through sustained 31mph pace.
EF Education used dual rider breakaway to control tempo instead of launching attacks, protecting sprinter through exhaustion rather than aggression.
Multiple crashes in peloton on stage 19 reveal deep fatigue affecting bike handling after consecutive mountain stages.

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: third stage win demonstrates repeatable breakaway sprint speed even late in grand tour
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: sustained breakaway pace-making at 31mph with final leadout shows exceptional grand tour diesel engine
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: positive: able to accelerate and contest sprint on stage 19 signals improved form trajectory exiting Vuelta
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: team required zero domestique work on breakaway stage, conserving resources before final time trial
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: climbing gains appear to have cost sprint speed, highlighting sprinter power-to-weight tradeoff
